title: Comstock Resources (NYSE:CRK) Lowered to Sell Rating by Wall Street Zen
summary: Wall Street Zen has downgraded Comstock Resources (NYSE: CRK) from a "hold" to a "sell" rating, with a consensus "Reduce" rating and an average price target of $20.88 among analysts. Despite beating EPS and revenue estimates, the stock opened at $16.95, trading below its 50- and 200-day moving averages, and a VP sold shares while some institutions increased their holdings. The company reported strong Q4 2025 earnings with revenue up 115.5% year-over-year.

title: DTE Energy (NYSE:DTE) Rating Increased to Hold at Wall Street Zen
summary: Wall Street Zen has upgraded DTE Energy (NYSE:DTE) from a "sell" to a "hold" rating. The utility provider beat quarterly earnings per share and revenue expectations, and multiple brokers have raised price targets, contributing to an average analyst rating of "Moderate Buy" with a target price of $155.23. Institutional investors and hedge funds hold a significant portion of the company's stock, with Vanguard Group Inc., Capital Research Global Investors, and State Street Corp among those increasing their holdings.

title: Cathie Wood’s ARK buys Amazon stock, sells AMD and Rocket Lab By Investing.com
summary: Cathie Wood's ARK ETFs made significant portfolio adjustments on Friday, April 24th, 2026. The firm acquired over 4 million shares of X-Energy Inc (NASDAQ:XE) and 280,450 shares of Amazon.com Inc (NASDAQ:AMZN), continuing a trend of increasing its Amazon holdings. Conversely, ARK sold off substantial portions of Advanced Micro Devices Inc (NASDAQ:AMD), Rocket Lab Corp (NASDAQ:RKLB), Teradyne Inc (NASDAQ:TER), Caterpillar Inc (NYSE:CAT), and Iridium Communications Inc (NASDAQ:IRDM), indicating a strategic shift in its investment focus.

title: [Form 4] PETROBRAS - PETROLEO BRASILEIRO SA Insider Trading Activity
summary: Clarice Coppetti, Chief Corporate Affairs Officer of PETROBRAS - PETROLEO BRASILEIRO SA (PBR), reported updated holdings of phantom shares. These phantom shares are linked to PETR3 common shares and were credited as a result of a dividend payment on April 22, 2026, under the Petrobras Performance Award Program. The awards are settled in cash upon vesting, with deferred portions vesting in four equal annual installments, and also receive proportional credits for dividends or interest on equity.

title: Brazil's Petrobras to raise jet fuel prices to distributors by 18% in May, reports Valor
summary: Brazilian state-run oil firm Petrobras is set to increase the average price of jet fuel sold to distributors by 18% starting May 1, according to local newspaper Valor Economico. This follows a 54.8% hike in April due to rising oil prices linked to the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran, with Petrobras allowing distributors to pay the increase in installments. The company adjusts jet fuel prices monthly based on factors like oil prices and currency exchange rates.

title: Zeo Energy Receives Nasdaq Notice on Minimum Bid Price
summary: Zeo Energy (ZEO) has received a notice from Nasdaq for failing to meet the minimum $1 bid price requirement for 30 consecutive business days. The company now has a 180-day period until October 20, 2026, to regain compliance, or it risks potential delisting. TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates ZEO as "Neutral" due to weak financial performance, including negative cash flow, declining revenue, and widening losses.

title: Weekly Market Update: Stocks Flat as Energy Rises, Healthcare Falls
summary: The Morningstar US Market Index saw a slight rise of 0.46% for the week ending April 24, with energy and technology sectors performing best, while healthcare and communication services declined. Arm Holdings and Advanced Micro Devices were among the top stock gainers, while Charter Communications and Medpace Holdings were among the top losers. The report also highlights key economic events and earnings reports scheduled for the upcoming week.

title: Plug Power Stock Watch: Louisiana Hydrogen Plant Hits 90% Availability As Cash Burn Test Looms
summary: Plug Power’s St. Gabriel, Louisiana hydrogen facility, a joint venture with Olin, achieved 90% availability in Q1, producing 448.3 metric tons of liquid hydrogen and filling 101 trailers. This performance is crucial for Plug to demonstrate network scalability, improve margins, and reduce cash burn, especially after a positive gross margin in Q4. Despite these operational improvements, the company's stock, along with other hydrogen and fuel-cell stocks, experienced a slight dip, and Plug Power faces ongoing financial risks and hurdles as it aims for sustained profitability and efficient liquidity management.

title: Uranium Energy Corp (UEC) Stock Down 6.0% but Still Overvalued -- GF Score: 51/100
summary: Uranium Energy Corp (UEC) shares fell 6.0% to $14.05, yet the stock remains significantly overvalued with a GF Value of $1.99, indicating a 606.0% overvaluation. The company's GF Score is 51/100, highlighting strong financial strength but weak profitability and growth. Insiders have shown no recent activity, suggesting a neutral outlook on the company's immediate future.

title: Suncor Energy Inc. stock falls Friday, underperforms market
summary: Suncor Energy Inc. (SU) shares dropped 1.04% to C$87.56 on Friday, underperforming the broader Canadian market as the S&P/TSX Composite Index fell 0.03%. The company's stock closed 7.2% below its 52-week high of C$94.34, which was reached on March 30th.

title: Scorpio Tankers leads seven ocean transportation stocks with new Buy ratings at Jefferies
summary: Jefferies has initiated Buy ratings on seven marine transportation stocks, citing rising energy prices due to the war in Iran as a key driver for the sector's bullish sentiment. Among the favored stocks are International Seaways and Scorpio Tankers, noted for their strong balance sheets, strategic asset exposure, and attractive valuations. Geopolitical events are contributing to a tight supply environment, making the sector outlook appealing.

title: Why ConocoPhillips (COP) Stock Is Trading Lower Today
summary: Shares of ConocoPhillips (COP) dropped 2.9% after crude oil prices eased due to signs of de-escalation in the Middle East, specifically a three-week ceasefire extension between Israel and Lebanon and reports of potential peace talks involving Iran. This news reduced concerns about supply disruptions, leading to lower oil prices and subsequently impacting the profitability and stock value of energy companies like ConocoPhillips. The stock closed down 2.2% at $121.66.

title: Houston's oil field services companies see double-digit drops in Middle East revenue
summary: Houston's three major oil field services companies—Halliburton, SLB, and Baker Hughes—experienced significant double-digit declines in Middle East revenue during the first quarter of 2026. This downturn is primarily attributed to regional conflicts, with forecasts suggesting further revenue decreases in the second quarter if the current geopolitical situation persists.

title: APA Corporation (APA) Stock Trades Down, Here Is Why
summary: Shares of APA Corporation (APA), an oil and gas producer, fell 2.9% after crude oil prices dropped due to hopes of diplomatic progress between the US and Iran. As a result, WTI crude oil futures slipped to $94.8 per barrel, impacting APA’s financial performance which is closely tied to energy prices. This decline follows a previous drop related to a potential US-Iran ceasefire and a bearish IEA report forecasting a contraction in global oil demand.

title: Brookfield Renewable Partners (TSE:BEP.UN) Stock Price Expected to Rise, Desjardins Analyst Says
summary: Desjardins has raised its price target for Brookfield Renewable Partners (TSE:BEP.UN) from C$45.00 to C$48.00, maintaining a "hold" rating and suggesting a potential upside of 3.34%. Despite this positive outlook, the company faces profitability challenges with a negative net margin of 4.2% and high leverage, indicated by a debt-to-equity ratio of 107.8. Brookfield Renewable, which operates a globally diversified portfolio of clean energy assets, reported C$0.74 EPS for the quarter and has a market cap of C$14.21 billion.

title: A Look At National Fuel Gas (NFG) Valuation As Analyst Optimism Builds Ahead March 2026 Earnings
summary: National Fuel Gas (NFG) is attracting investor attention ahead of its March 2026 earnings, with analysts optimistic about its performance. The company is currently trading at $89.15, below the most-followed fair value estimate of $102, suggesting it might be undervalued based on its strong Marcellus Shale inventory and efficient production. However, a discounted cash flow model by Simply Wall St offers a lower fair value of $80.84, indicating a potential overvaluation by the market.

title: Middle East Issues Hit SLB Operations, Profit
summary: SLB reported a challenging first quarter of 2026, with revenue beating estimates but GAAP EPS decreasing due to widespread disruptions in the Middle East. Geopolitical issues led to demobilization of operations in several countries, particularly impacting Well Construction and Reservoir Performance segments. Despite these headwinds, the company saw growth in Digital revenue and Production Systems (driven by ChampionX acquisition), and advanced strategic moves including a share repurchase program, dividend approval, and an agreement to acquire S&P Global's geoscience and petroleum engineering software portfolio.

title: FuelCell Energy (NASDAQ:FCEL) Stock Price Down 11.2% - Here's Why
summary: FuelCell Energy (NASDAQ:FCEL) shares dropped 11.2% to $10.81 on Friday due to weakened analyst sentiment and mixed quarterly results. Analysts have a "Reduce" consensus rating with a target price of $8.24, with recent cuts from Jefferies and Wells Fargo.

title: NRG Energy Inc. stock outperforms competitors on strong trading day
summary: Shares of NRG Energy Inc. (NRG) increased by 3.42%, closing at $159.81 on Friday. The article highlights NRG's outperformance relative to its competitors and the broader market trends.

title: 3 Cruise Stocks to Buy as Oil Prices Plummet
summary: The cruise industry, despite a recent downturn due to the US-Iran conflict impacting fuel prices and travel routes, is poised for a rebound as oil prices are expected to stabilize. This article highlights three cruise stocks—Carnival Corp. (CCL), Royal Caribbean Group (RCL), and Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings (NCLH)—as strong "buy the dip" opportunities for investors taking a long-term view. Positive financial outlooks, strong customer bookings, strategic fleet expansions, and analyst endorsements suggest significant upside potential for these companies once the geopolitical situation calms and fuel costs decrease.

summary: Algonquin Power & Utilities Corp. (AQN) stock remained steady on Friday despite a negative trading session for the Canadian market, where the S&P/TSX Composite Index fell 0.03%. title: OGE Benefiting From Energy Demand Growth
summary: OGE Energy, having completed its transition to a fully regulated electric utility in late 2022, now bases its growth prospects primarily on Oklahoma rate regulation and increasing electricity demand. The company's earnings power is also supported by infrastructure investments and constructive regulatory developments, boosting investment for 2026-2029.

title: Alpha Metallurgical Resources: Cyclical Opportunity, India-Linked Demand Tailwinds
summary: Alpha Metallurgical Resources (AMR) is highlighted as the leading U.S. producer of premium coking coal, with a significant portion of its exports going to India, a growing market for metallurgical coal imports. The company is noted for its strong capital allocation strategy, including share buybacks that have reduced its share count by approximately 30%, and is seen as a buy due to favorable risk-reward prospects and demand tailwinds. The article suggests AMR offers a strong cash flow yield and potential for significant gains if met coal prices rise.

title: Constellation Energy vs. NextEra Energy: Which Utility Stock Wins Now?
summary: This article compares NextEra Energy (NEE) and Constellation Energy (CEG), two leading utility stocks, evaluating them based on earnings estimates, return on equity, dividend yield, capital investment plans, valuation, and price performance. While both companies are significant players in clean energy, the analysis concludes that NextEra Energy currently offers a stronger investment opportunity due to better earnings estimate movement, a higher dividend yield, more extensive capital investments, and a cheaper valuation. The article highlights the growing demand for clean electricity driven by AI, EVs, and industrial growth, and how both companies are investing in renewable and carbon-free technologies.

title: Scotiabank Raises Chevron (CVX) Target, Sees Mixed Trends Across Energy Sector
summary: Scotiabank increased its price target for Chevron (CVX) to $187 from $168, maintaining a Sector Perform rating, while noting mixed trends across the energy sector. The firm's outlook is generally above consensus for E&P companies but below for independent refiners. Chevron recently restored full production at its Wheatstone LNG facility in Western Australia after cyclone damage, which had temporarily taken processing trains offline.

title: ExxonMobil's 2025 Giving Campaign delivers record results to United Way of Greater Houston and Houston community
summary: ExxonMobil's 2025 Houston Giving Campaign raised a record $16 million, marking the largest fundraising total in over 60 years of community giving for the company. This amount includes contributions from employees, retirees, the ExxonMobil Foundation, and various fundraising events, showcasing the company's commitment to strengthening the Greater Houston community. Of the total, $13.3 million was directed to United Way of Greater Houston, supporting education, workforce development, and essential family services, while additional funds benefited STEM scholarships and local nonprofits.

title: A Look At Valaris (VAL) Valuation After Strong 1 Year Returns And Mixed Near Term Momentum
summary: Valaris (VAL) has shown strong returns over the past year and three months, but its near-term momentum is fading. Despite trading 37% above a fair value estimate of $65.93, driven by a large contract backlog, its current P/E ratio of 6.4x appears low compared to industry averages. Investors are presented with mixed signals regarding its valuation, urging a closer look at underlying data and risks.

title: Centrus Energy (LEU) Maintained by B. Riley Securities -- Price Target Lowered to $295
summary: B. Riley Securities has maintained a 'Buy' rating for Centrus Energy (LEU) but lowered its price target from $315.00 to $295.00. Despite the 'Buy' rating, GuruFocus indicates that LEU is significantly overvalued at 306.1% above its intrinsic value and has a moderate GF Score™ of 66/100, suggesting caution for investors due to high valuation metrics and limited insider activity.

title: Why Is Exxon So Afraid of NLPC’s Proposal to Split Chair and CEO?
summary: The National Legal and Policy Center (NLPC) is questioning Exxon Mobil's strong opposition to its shareholder proposal which seeks to split the roles of Chair and CEO. NLPC criticizes Exxon's proxy statement for labeling the proposal a "zombie" and the NLPC as a "serial proponent," arguing that Exxon's current CEO, Darren Woods, has made questionable strategic decisions and cultivated an adversarial relationship with a pro-hydrocarbon administration. The article suggests that Exxon's defensive rhetoric indicates the importance of passing the proposal, aligning with a growing corporate governance norm where 60% of S&P 500 companies separate these roles.

title: SLB rises following in-line Q1; to acquire S&P Global’s subsurface oil software business (SLB:NYSE)
summary: SLB (NYSE:SLB) shares rose 1.9% after the company reported Q1 adjusted earnings that matched analyst expectations, despite a year-over-year decline attributed to disruptions in the Middle East. The oilfield services giant also announced its intention to acquire S&P Global's subsurface oil software business, a move expected to enhance its technology and competitiveness, particularly in the U.S. shale market. SLB anticipates crude prices will remain elevated even after the resolution of the Middle East conflict.

title: Constellation Energy vs. NextEra Energy: Which Utility Stock Wins Now?
summary: This article compares NextEra Energy (NEE) and Constellation Energy (CEG), two utility stocks in the Electric Power industry, to determine which offers a stronger investment opportunity. Both companies are investing heavily in clean energy, with NEE focusing on wind and solar and CEG on nuclear power. While both are rated "Hold" by Zacks, NEE is presented as having an edge due to better price performance, earnings estimate movement, dividend yield, capital investment plans, and a slightly cheaper valuation.

title: ExxonMobil’s Darren Woods: The Case for an Independent Chair
summary: The National Legal and Policy Center (NLPC) is urging ExxonMobil shareholders to vote for a proposal to separate the roles of Chairman and CEO, currently held by Darren Woods. The NLPC cites Woods's public stances on the Paris Agreement and Venezuela, which have led to significant financial setbacks and political friction, as reasons for needing an independent board chair. They also note that 60% of S&P 500 companies have already adopted this separation, and BlackRock, ExxonMobil's second-largest shareholder, had previously supported such a split.

title: Royal Bank Of Canada Forecasts Strong Price Appreciation for Patterson-UTI Energy (NASDAQ:PTEN) Stock
summary: Royal Bank of Canada has increased its price target for Patterson-UTI Energy (NASDAQ:PTEN) stock from $12 to $14, maintaining an "outperform" rating and implying a 23% upside. This follows the company's Q1 results beating revenue and EPS estimates, and a declared quarterly dividend of $0.10. Despite these positive indicators, PTEN faces challenges including a 12.7% year-over-year revenue decline, negative net margin and ROE, and projected negative full-year EPS, indicating continued profitability concerns.

title: Alliance Resource earnings on deck: Coal prices weigh on outlook By Investing.com
summary: Alliance Resource Partners LP is scheduled to report first-quarter earnings, with analysts anticipating a significant drop in profits due to challenging coal prices. Investors will be closely watching for signs of operational efficiency improvements and updates on the Mettiki mine's future to offset pricing headwinds. Despite a challenging environment, two analysts rate the stock a Strong Buy, suggesting potential upside.

title: Earnings Preview: Imperial Oil (IMO) Q1 Earnings Expected to Decline
summary: Imperial Oil (IMO) is expected to report a year-over-year decline in Q1 earnings despite higher revenues, with the report anticipated on May 1st. Historically, Imperial Oil has consistently beaten EPS estimates, doing so four times in the last four quarters.

title: Chevron CEO Expects Air Travel To 'Get Worse' Over The Next Few Weeks As Hormuz Crisis Pushes Fuel Costs Higher
summary: Chevron CEO Mike Wirth predicts a worsening situation for air travel in the coming weeks due to the Hormuz crisis, leading to higher fuel costs and reduced flight availability. He notes that the jet fuel market is tightening, particularly in Europe and Asia, and North American jet fuel prices have surged over 80% year-on-year. Wirth also highlighted that U.S. airlines are relatively better off than their European counterparts as the U.S. produces its own jet fuel.

title: U S Global Investors Inc. Takes $1.12 Million Position in Viking Holdings Ltd. $VIK

title: Jet fuel climbs above $3.80 a gallon as XCF makes its SAF case

title: Halliburton Q1 2026: $5.4B Revenue Holds as Middle East Conflict Dents EPS

title: GeoPark (NYSE: GPRK) details 1Q 2026 output, prices and new drilling

title: Cenovus Energy (NYSE:CVE) Rating Increased to Strong-Buy at Zacks Research

title: Future of Solar Panel Market 2026 | Market is set to reach US$ 901.9
